name: Front
one_liner: Front is a customer communication/shared-inbox helpdesk platform with a comprehensive REST Core API for managing conversations, contacts, messages, tags, and more.
access_notes: Front is a paid SaaS with a free trial; no free API-only dev tier. API tokens are self-serve and generated instantly from Settings > Developers > API Tokens, but you must be a workspace admin to create/manage them. OAuth is required for public/partner integrations. No human approval needed to obtain credentials within your own instance.
api_notes: Front Core API is a REST backend giving comprehensive control over all Front entities (Conversations, Messages, Comments, Contacts, Contact Groups, Tags, Inboxes, Channels, Teammates, Drafts, Events, Attachments, Analytics, etc.), easily 100+ operations. Bearer token auth in Authorization header. Overall plus per-endpoint burst rate limits apply.
mcp_notes: Front ships an official MCP server (open beta) at mcp.frontapp.com/mcp — a single OAuth-authenticated endpoint where agents act as a specific Front user with that user's permissions. Documented at dev.frontapp.com/docs/mcp-server. Community/third-party MCP servers (e.g. zqushair/Frontapp-MCP, Pipedream, Zapier) also exist.
main_blocker: None significant — public REST API with self-serve credentials. Minor gates: must be an admin to mint API tokens, and Front itself requires a paid plan/trial (no free-forever tier).

evidence.claim: Front Core API uses Bearer tokens and supports OAuth 2.0 (authorization code grant) as well as API tokens for authentication.
API tokens are created in Settings > Developers > API Tokens; you must be an admin to create or manage API tokens.
Front's Core API is a REST backend giving comprehensive control over all entities from Contacts to Comments, with overall and burst rate limits.
Front offers an official MCP server (open beta) at mcp.frontapp.com/mcp using a single OAuth-authenticated endpoint scoped to a Front user's permissions.

open_questions: Exact minimum paid plan tier required for API access is not stated in dev docs; whether any endpoints are gated behind higher (Scale/Premier) plans is unconfirmed.
